title: Merge-risk blacklist membership for we:package.json + we:.eleventy.js — keyed manifests vs the line-structured-optimistic principle
summary: Prep research for decision #2149 (are we:package.json and we:.eleventy.js merge-risk blacklist files forcing same-lane packing / pre-lock / drain serial-replay, or does #1952's 'build config is line-structured -> optimistic' principle cover them; report we:reports/2026-07-02-merge-risk-blacklist-package-json-eleventy.md). IN-REPO GROUNDING: RESERVED_MERGE_RISK_BY_REPO lives canonically in we:scripts/readiness/lane-partition.mjs (tested) + the inline mirror in we:.claude/skills/batch-backlog-items/parallel-execute.workflow.js; membership drives partition rule 2c (same-lane), the file-locks pre-lock planner, and (since #2138 Fork 3, ratified 2026-07-02) drain-time denylist-overlap serial-replay. TWO GROUNDING CORRECTIONS: (1) neither path was EVER in the list (git log -S shows no entry added/removed) — #1952's ratified removals were we:tsconfig.json + we:vite.config.mts; we:package.json is swept in only by the header comment's generalized 'BUILD CONFIG' parenthetical, so a GO narrows a codified GENERALIZATION, not the ratified removals; (2) the clean-but-wrong case for keyed manifests is real but narrow — two lanes adding the SAME key at different offsets git-merge clean into a duplicate-key object (npm/JSON last-wins, silent; no gate parses for dup keys), while distinct-key adds merge clean AND correct. EVIDENCE: batch-2026-07-01-wf flagged 7 multiLaneFiles incl. #2018+#2020 both appending to we:.eleventy.js and #2024+#2087 both appending to we:package.json (post-hoc notices, no wrong merge observed); churn since 2026-06-01: package manifest 47 commits / eleventy config 22 vs tsconfig 2. SIBLING RESHAPE: #2138's ratified Fork-3 amendment whitelists exactly these files' additive regions (script-key append / per-entry registration line), rules dep adds NOT whitelisted -> serial-replay, and states it 'Composes with #2149 (declares the irreducible residual)' — but the carve is CONDITIONAL ('iff on the denylist'): for an unlisted file it goes dormant, not contradicted, and the 'Composes with' line is a compositional forward-note to a then-preparing item, not authority (citation-scope check); under the deferred drain, landing is serial anyway so a blacklist entry costs production-lane concurrency only. #2123 (all sessions in lane clones) RAISES exposure. PRIOR ART: CODEOWNERS/Zuul path-scoped serialization (= the ratified #1935 static-denylist hybrid); npm-merge-driver (regenerate manifest/lockfile on conflict -> package-lock is category-(2) derived, no separate entry); jsonc duplicate-key linting (a deterministic post-merge guard alternative); ParallelChange judged-additive (already in #2138). SYNTHESIS (post-skeptic — the two files are NOT symmetric): Fork 1 we:package.json = (a) bare optimistic vs (b) declare (3) vs (c) optimistic + deterministic DUPLICATE-KEY LINT in the per-merge gate, default (c) — skeptic REFUTED the draft blacklist default: for a keyed manifest the entire clean-but-wrong class is the duplicate key, which is script-decidable, so a gate rule converts silent-wrong to gate-red -> serial-replay with strictly broader coverage than lane-only serialization (also guards solo/human/drain merges); (b)'s residual is replay-speed = prioritization (#1961); lint ships in the ruling's own changeset; package-lock gets no entry (a lockfile clash is a real conflict that replays). Fork 2 we:.eleventy.js = (a) optimistic vs (b) declare (3) NOW, default (b) — SURVIVES-WITH-AMENDMENT: registration-monolith failure class is NOT lintable (computed/wrapped names; ordering/side-effect sensitivity), so the pre-emptive entry is the only closing mechanism; the draft's 'ratify the fragment split as end-state' branch was struck (category (1) already blesses split-and-exit generically — pre-blessing a future story is ordering), delisting conditional on a split proving order-insensitivity. ALSO SKEPTIC-SURFACED: the statute anchor's literal '(3) ... build config -> locked' text already contradicts the ratified #1952 removals (which live only in the code header) — the reconciling anchor rewrite is owed under ANY ruling. Layer: agent-workflow / session-tooling (the #1935/#2123/#2138 cluster) — nothing crosses the WE<->FUI standard boundary.
